records.sprk_subject: Meridian Corp v. Pinnacle Industries — Engagement and Case Strategy
records.sprk_body:
Dear Sarah,

Thank you for selecting Baker & Associates to represent Meridian Corp in this matter. We are confident in the strength of Meridian's patent portfolio and the merits of the infringement claims against Pinnacle Industries.

I wanted to follow up on our initial meeting and confirm the key points of our litigation strategy. First, we will file the complaint in the Northern District of California, asserting infringement of Patents 10,234,567, 10,456,789, and 10,678,901. Based on our preliminary analysis, the AutoForge product line appears to incorporate the core precision alignment mechanisms covered by these patents.

Second, we should anticipate that Pinnacle will assert invalidity defenses based on prior art. Our initial prior art search suggests these defenses are manageable, but we will need to engage a technical expert early to strengthen our position.

Third, I recommend budgeting approximately $500,000 through trial, with the expectation that settlement discussions may reduce the total spend. We will provide detailed monthly invoices and quarterly budget reviews.

Please let me know if you have any questions. We will schedule a strategy meeting next week to discuss the discovery plan in detail.

Best regards,
Rachel Torres
Lead Partner, IP Litigation
Baker & Associates LLP
records.sprk_description: Initial engagement letter and case strategy overview from Rachel Torres to Sarah Chen outlining litigation plan, anticipated defenses, and budget framework.

records.sprk_subject: RE: Meridian Corp v. Pinnacle Industries — Engagement and Case Strategy
records.sprk_body:
Rachel,

Thank you for the thorough overview. The board has approved the litigation budget of $500,000 and I have full authority to direct strategy decisions on behalf of Meridian Corp.

A few points from our side:

First, James Morrison, our VP of Engineering, was directly involved in developing the patented processes and will be an excellent fact witness. He is eager to assist and can be made available for preparation sessions on short notice.

Second, we are particularly concerned about the AutoForge Mark III model, which was released just two months ago. Our engineering team believes it incorporates even more of our patented technology than the earlier models. Please ensure this is captured in the complaint.

Third, I want to understand our settlement posture early. What range do you think is reasonable given the revenue Pinnacle has generated from AutoForge? Our internal estimates suggest AutoForge has generated approximately $180M in revenue over the past three years.

Looking forward to the strategy meeting next week.

Best,
Sarah Chen
General Counsel
Meridian Corp
records.sprk_description: Sarah Chen confirms board approval of litigation budget and provides key information about James Morrison as fact witness, AutoForge Mark III concerns, and settlement range inquiry.

records.sprk_subject: Document Preservation and Collection — Meridian Internal Sources
records.sprk_body:
Sarah,

As we discussed in our strategy meeting, we need to initiate document preservation and collection from Meridian's internal systems. I am writing to formalize the litigation hold requirements.

Please ensure the following categories of documents are preserved and collected:

1. All engineering notebooks, lab records, and technical specifications related to the patented manufacturing processes (Patents 10,234,567, 10,456,789, and 10,678,901).
2. All communications between Meridian employees regarding Pinnacle Industries, the AutoForge product line, or competitive intelligence.
3. All patent prosecution files maintained by your in-house IP team.
4. All licensing agreements, royalty discussions, or partnership proposals involving the three asserted patents.

Lisa Huang, our senior paralegal, will coordinate directly with your IT department on the collection logistics. We anticipate the initial collection will take approximately two weeks.

Please confirm that a litigation hold notice has been issued to all relevant custodians within Meridian.

Best regards,
Michael Park
Senior Associate, IP Litigation
Baker & Associates LLP
records.sprk_description: Michael Park outlines document preservation and collection requirements to Sarah Chen, requesting litigation hold confirmation and identifying document categories needed for discovery.

records.sprk_subject: RE: Document Preservation and Collection — Meridian Internal Sources
records.sprk_body:
Michael,

Confirmed. Our IT department issued the litigation hold notice to all relevant custodians on August 26, the day after our strategy meeting. The hold covers 14 custodians including James Morrison, our CTO, and the three senior engineers on the precision manufacturing team.

I have asked our IT director to coordinate with Lisa Huang on collection scheduling. Please have her contact Mark Stevens at mstevens@example.com to set up the initial meetings.

One question — should we also preserve marketing materials that reference the patented technology? Our marketing team created several white papers and product sheets that describe the precision alignment mechanisms in some detail. These could be helpful in establishing the value of the patents.

Best,
Sarah
records.sprk_description: Sarah Chen confirms litigation hold has been issued to 14 custodians and provides IT contact for collection coordination. Asks about preserving marketing materials.

records.sprk_subject: Discovery Scheduling — Proposed ESI Protocol
records.sprk_body:
Dear Sarah,

I wanted to update you on the discovery scheduling discussions with opposing counsel. We have exchanged proposed ESI protocols with Chen Law Group, and there are several points of disagreement.

The main disputes center on: (1) the scope of custodians — Pinnacle wants to limit production to only three custodians, while we have identified at least seven key individuals; (2) search terms — they object to several of our proposed terms as overbroad; and (3) the format of production — they want to produce in native format only, while we need certain documents in TIFF format for our review platform.

We have scheduled a meet and confer for October 30, and if we cannot resolve these issues, I will file a motion to compel compliance with our proposed protocol. Based on our experience with Judge Sullivan, she tends to favor broader discovery in patent cases.

I will keep you updated on the outcome of the meet and confer.

Best regards,
Rachel Torres
records.sprk_description: Rachel Torres updates Sarah Chen on ESI protocol disputes with opposing counsel regarding custodian scope, search terms, and production format. Meet and confer scheduled.

records.sprk_subject: Deposition Preparation — James Morrison
records.sprk_body:
Sarah,

We have finalized the deposition date for James Morrison — November 18, 2025, at our offices. I want to schedule three preparation sessions with James to ensure he is thoroughly prepared for the defense examination.

Proposed preparation schedule:
- Session 1: November 10 (2 hours) — Review of key documents and chronology
- Session 2: November 13 (3 hours) — Practice questioning on technical topics
- Session 3: November 17 (2 hours) — Final preparation and review of potential traps

The defense will likely focus on the timeline of development, any prior art references James may have been aware of, and the specific technical differences between Meridian's patented processes and the prior art. We need James to be comfortable discussing the technical details without volunteering information beyond what is asked.

Please confirm James's availability for these sessions and let me know if he has any concerns.

Best regards,
Michael Park
records.sprk_description: Michael Park schedules three deposition preparation sessions for James Morrison ahead of his November 18 deposition, outlining likely defense questioning areas.

records.sprk_subject: Case Update — November 2025
records.sprk_body:
Dear Sarah,

I am writing to provide our monthly case update for November 2025.

Discovery Progress: We completed James Morrison's deposition on November 18. He performed well under examination and effectively described the development timeline and unique aspects of the patented processes. Opposing counsel spent considerable time on prior art references, but James handled these questions carefully.

Pinnacle's second document production is due December 1, and we expect approximately 3,000 additional documents including AutoForge engineering specifications.

We served our Rule 26(e) supplemental disclosures on November 28, adding two additional witnesses identified during document review.

Expert Engagement: We have formally engaged Dr. Robert Patel to serve as our technical expert on manufacturing process infringement. He has begun his analysis and will deliver an initial report by early February.

Budget: November spend was approximately $63,840, bringing our cumulative total to approximately $173,190. This is consistent with our budget projections for the discovery phase.

Next month we expect to focus on Pinnacle's second production, preparation for the Thomas Wright deposition, and continued claim construction analysis.

Please let me know if you have any questions or would like to discuss any of these items.

Best regards,
Rachel Torres
records.sprk_description: Monthly case update covering Morrison deposition completion, supplemental disclosures, expert engagement of Dr. Patel, and November budget status.

records.sprk_subject: RE: Case Update — November 2025
records.sprk_body:
Rachel,

Thank you for the thorough update. I have shared the key points with our executive team and they are pleased with the progress.

Two items I want to flag:

1. James mentioned to me that during his deposition, opposing counsel asked several questions about a specific engineering meeting in March 2024 where the precision alignment concept was first discussed. James felt the questions were probing whether the idea originated from a trade show presentation rather than internal R&D. Should we be concerned about this line of inquiry?

2. Our CFO has asked me to provide a forecast of total litigation costs through trial. Can you provide an updated projection at the Q4 budget review?

Looking forward to hearing about the December developments.

Best,
Sarah
records.sprk_description: Sarah Chen responds to monthly update, raising concerns about opposing counsel's line of questioning at Morrison deposition regarding origins of the patented concept, and requesting updated cost forecast.

records.sprk_subject: Settlement Posture Discussion — Confidential
records.sprk_body:
Sarah,

Following our call yesterday, I wanted to memorialize our discussion regarding settlement posture.

Based on our analysis of Pinnacle's AutoForge revenue ($180M+ over three years), a reasonable royalty analysis, and the strength of our claim construction positions, we believe a settlement range of $15M-$25M is appropriate.

However, I recommend we hold off on making a formal demand until after the Markman hearing scheduled for February. A favorable claim construction ruling would significantly strengthen our negotiating position and could push the settlement value toward the higher end of the range.

Opposing counsel has made informal inquiries about our openness to mediation, which suggests Pinnacle may be feeling pressure. I recommend we respond that we are open to discussing resolution but are focused on preparing for trial in the near term.

Please confirm you are comfortable with this approach.

Best regards,
Rachel Torres
records.sprk_description: Confidential settlement strategy discussion. Rachel Torres recommends $15M-$25M range, advises waiting until after Markman hearing before making formal demand.

records.sprk_subject: RE: Settlement Posture Discussion — Confidential
records.sprk_body:
Rachel,

I agree with your recommendation. Let us wait for the Markman ruling before making a formal demand. The board is aligned on the $15M-$25M range as a starting point for discussions.

One additional thought — our CEO has mentioned that a licensing arrangement could also be acceptable if the terms are favorable. Rather than a one-time payment, Meridian might prefer an ongoing royalty of 4-6% on AutoForge revenue. This would provide a continuing revenue stream and acknowledge Meridian's IP rights going forward. Could you model both scenarios for the board presentation in January?

Best,
Sarah
records.sprk_description: Sarah Chen agrees with settlement timing strategy and introduces licensing arrangement as alternative resolution, requesting modeling of both lump-sum and royalty scenarios.

records.sprk_subject: Document Production Update — Privilege Issues
records.sprk_body:
Dear Sarah,

I wanted to bring to your attention a privilege issue that has arisen with Pinnacle's document production.

In reviewing Pinnacle's privilege log, we identified 12 entries where the privilege assertion appears questionable. Several documents appear to be business communications that do not involve legal advice, and Pinnacle may be improperly withholding them as attorney-client privilege.

We have raised this issue with opposing counsel in a letter dated December 10, and they have refused to produce the documents or provide additional detail on the privilege claims. Accordingly, we are preparing a motion to compel production and request in camera review by Judge Sullivan.

The hearing is likely to be scheduled for early January. I do not expect this to significantly impact the budget, as the motion is relatively straightforward.

Best regards,
Michael Park
records.sprk_description: Michael Park informs Sarah Chen about questionable privilege assertions in Pinnacle's production, advising that a motion to compel will be filed seeking in camera review.

records.sprk_subject: Post-Markman Hearing Analysis — Confidential
records.sprk_body:
Dear Sarah,

I am pleased to report that the Markman hearing on February 14 went very well for Meridian. Judge Sullivan construed 6 of 8 disputed claim terms in our favor.

Most significantly, the court adopted a broad construction of 'precision alignment mechanism,' which is the core limitation in all three asserted patents. This construction encompasses the full range of AutoForge implementations, including the Mark III model you were particularly concerned about.

The two terms construed in Pinnacle's favor are relatively minor and relate to peripheral claim limitations. Our infringement analysis remains strong under these constructions.

This ruling significantly strengthens our position for both trial and settlement purposes. I recommend we now consider making a formal settlement demand. Based on the favorable claim construction, I believe we can justify a demand at the higher end of our previously discussed range.

I would like to schedule a call with you and the executive team to discuss next steps. Are you available this week?

Best regards,
Rachel Torres
records.sprk_description: Rachel Torres reports favorable Markman ruling with 6 of 8 claim terms construed in Meridian's favor. Recommends making formal settlement demand at high end of range.

records.sprk_subject: Meridian Corp v. Pinnacle Industries — Meet and Confer Re: ESI Protocol
records.sprk_body:
Dear Mr. Kim,

I am writing to follow up on our telephone conference of October 30 regarding the parties' dispute over the ESI protocol.

As discussed, Meridian Corp maintains that production from a minimum of seven custodians is necessary to ensure complete discovery. The four additional custodians we have identified — including the AutoForge project lead and three senior engineers — are directly involved in the design and development of the accused products and are likely to possess highly relevant documents.

Regarding search terms, we are willing to narrow two of the disputed terms as follows: (1) 'alignment' will be paired with 'manufacturing OR precision OR mechanism' rather than standing alone; (2) 'AutoForge' will be limited to documents created after January 2023.

However, we cannot agree to limit production to native format only. Our review platform requires TIFF images with extracted text and metadata for efficient review.

If we cannot reach agreement within the next seven days, we intend to file a motion with the Court seeking resolution. Please respond at your earliest convenience.

Sincerely,
Rachel Torres
Baker & Associates LLP
records.sprk_description: Formal meet and confer letter to opposing counsel regarding ESI protocol disputes on custodian scope, search terms, and production format.

records.sprk_subject: RE: Meridian Corp v. Pinnacle Industries — Meet and Confer Re: ESI Protocol
records.sprk_body:
Dear Ms. Torres,

Thank you for your letter of November 3. Pinnacle Industries has carefully considered your proposals.

We are willing to add the AutoForge project lead as a fourth custodian, bringing our total to four custodians. However, we continue to object to producing from the three additional engineers you have identified, as their involvement in the accused products is peripheral and the burden of collecting and reviewing their documents would be disproportionate to the likely benefit.

We accept your narrowed search terms as described in your letter.

Regarding production format, we propose a compromise: we will produce engineering documents and source code in native format (which is necessary to preserve functionality) and all other documents in TIFF format with extracted text as you have requested.

We believe this is a reasonable compromise that balances the needs of both parties. If you are unable to agree, we are prepared to present our position to the Court.

Sincerely,
David Kim
Chen Law Group
records.sprk_description: Opposing counsel responds to ESI protocol meet and confer, agreeing to one additional custodian and narrowed search terms but maintaining objections to remaining custodians.

records.sprk_subject: Meridian Corp v. Pinnacle Industries — Deposition Scheduling
records.sprk_body:
Dear Ms. Torres,

I am writing regarding the scheduling of depositions in this matter.

Pinnacle Industries proposes the following deposition schedule:

1. James Morrison (Meridian VP Engineering) — November 18, 2025
2. Thomas Wright (Pinnacle CEO) — December 5, 2025
3. Amanda Foster (Pinnacle In-House Counsel) — January 14, 2026

We suggest all depositions be conducted at the respective counsel's offices for the convenience of the deponents. Mr. Wright and Ms. Foster depositions would take place at our Austin offices.

Please confirm whether these dates are acceptable. If not, please propose alternative dates within the same general timeframe.

Sincerely,
David Kim
Chen Law Group
records.sprk_description: Opposing counsel proposes deposition schedule for three fact witnesses: Morrison, Wright, and Foster.

records.sprk_subject: RE: Meridian Corp v. Pinnacle Industries — Deposition Scheduling
records.sprk_body:
Dear Mr. Kim,

Thank you for the proposed deposition schedule. We accept the dates for Mr. Morrison (November 18), Mr. Wright (December 5), and Ms. Foster (January 14).

We also wish to schedule depositions of two additional witnesses:

4. Dr. Robert Patel (Plaintiff's Expert) — to be scheduled after expert reports are exchanged, tentatively March 2026
5. Dr. Emily Nakamura (Defendant's Expert) — same timeframe

We will provide specific dates for expert depositions once the expert disclosure schedule is confirmed.

Please confirm receipt and let us know if the proposed arrangement for fact witness depositions is agreeable.

Sincerely,
Rachel Torres
Baker & Associates LLP
records.sprk_description: Rachel Torres accepts proposed deposition dates and identifies two additional expert depositions to be scheduled in March 2026.

records.sprk_subject: Meridian Corp v. Pinnacle Industries — Settlement Discussion Inquiry
records.sprk_body:
Dear Ms. Torres,

I am writing on an informal and without-prejudice basis to inquire whether Meridian Corp would be amenable to exploring settlement discussions in this matter.

As you know, patent litigation of this nature is costly for both parties, and our client believes that an early resolution could be mutually beneficial. While Pinnacle Industries continues to maintain its defenses, we recognize that both parties have legitimate interests that might be better served through negotiation.

If Meridian is open to discussions, we would propose an initial meeting between counsel to explore the parameters for a potential resolution. We are also open to engaging a mediator if the parties prefer a structured process.

Please let me know your client's position at your convenience.

Sincerely,
David Kim
Chen Law Group
records.sprk_description: Opposing counsel makes informal settlement inquiry, suggesting both parties explore resolution discussions or mediation.

records.sprk_subject: RE: Meridian Corp v. Pinnacle Industries — Settlement Discussion Inquiry
records.sprk_body:
Dear Mr. Kim,

Thank you for your letter. Meridian Corp is always open to resolving disputes efficiently and recognizes the value of early engagement on potential resolution.

That said, we believe it would be premature to enter formal settlement discussions at this stage. Several key events remain outstanding, including claim construction and the completion of expert discovery, which will provide both parties with a more informed basis for evaluating the case.

We suggest revisiting this topic after the Markman hearing, tentatively scheduled for February 2026. At that point, both parties will have a clearer understanding of the claim scope and can have more productive discussions.

We remain focused on vigorous trial preparation while keeping the door open for resolution at the appropriate time.

Sincerely,
Rachel Torres
Baker & Associates LLP
records.sprk_description: Rachel Torres responds to settlement inquiry, acknowledging openness to resolution but suggesting discussions after the February Markman hearing.

records.sprk_subject: Meridian Corp v. Pinnacle Industries — Claim Construction Briefing Schedule
records.sprk_body:
Dear Ms. Torres,

Following the Court's scheduling order of January 5, I am writing to confirm the agreed-upon claim construction briefing schedule:

- Opening briefs: January 20, 2026
- Responsive briefs: February 3, 2026
- Reply briefs: February 10, 2026
- Hearing: February 14, 2026

Each party may address up to 8 disputed claim terms. We will circulate our proposed list of disputed terms by January 10.

Please confirm this schedule is agreeable.

Sincerely,
David Kim
Chen Law Group
records.sprk_description: Opposing counsel confirms claim construction briefing schedule leading to February 14 Markman hearing.

records.sprk_subject: Meridian Corp v. Pinnacle Industries — Post-Markman Settlement Dialogue
records.sprk_body:
Dear Ms. Torres,

In light of the Court's claim construction order, we write to revisit the possibility of settlement discussions.

Pinnacle Industries is prepared to engage in substantive discussions regarding a potential resolution. While we continue to maintain our invalidity defenses, we recognize that the Court's claim construction rulings provide a clearer framework for evaluating the parties' respective positions.

We propose a without-prejudice meeting between lead counsel in the second quarter of 2026, potentially with the assistance of a neutral mediator. Please let us know whether Meridian would be interested in exploring this path.

Sincerely,
David Kim
Chen Law Group
records.sprk_description: Post-Markman settlement inquiry from opposing counsel. Pinnacle signals willingness for substantive settlement discussions in Q2 2026, potentially with mediator.

records.sprk_subject: Expert Engagement — Dr. Robert Patel
records.sprk_body:
Dear Dr. Patel,

Thank you for agreeing to serve as plaintiff's expert witness in Meridian Corp v. Pinnacle Industries, Case No. 3:2025-cv-04892.

As we discussed, we need your expert opinion on whether Pinnacle Industries' AutoForge product line infringes Meridian Corp's Patents 10,234,567, 10,456,789, and 10,678,901. Specifically, we need you to:

1. Analyze the technical specifications of the AutoForge manufacturing system
2. Compare the AutoForge implementation against each claim of the asserted patents
3. Provide a claim-by-claim infringement analysis
4. Prepare a written expert report suitable for submission to the Court

We are sending you under separate cover the complete patent files, Pinnacle's document production (including AutoForge technical specifications), and the deposition transcripts of James Morrison and Thomas Wright.

Our target for your initial report is early February 2026. Please let us know your availability for a kickoff meeting next week.

Best regards,
Rachel Torres
Baker & Associates LLP
records.sprk_description: Formal expert engagement letter to Dr. Patel outlining scope of analysis, deliverables, and timeline for initial expert report.

records.sprk_subject: RE: Expert Engagement — Dr. Robert Patel
records.sprk_body:
Dear Rachel,

Thank you for the detailed engagement outline. I am prepared to begin work immediately upon receipt of the materials.

I am available for a kickoff meeting on December 2 at 10:00 AM Pacific. I suggest we use that session to walk through the patents in detail and identify the key claim limitations that will be the focus of my analysis.

Based on my initial review of the patent abstracts, I believe the precision alignment mechanism claims will be the most critical to the infringement analysis. I will need detailed technical specifications of the AutoForge system to assess element-by-element correspondence.

My rate remains $750 per hour as previously agreed, with an estimated total engagement of 80-100 hours through report delivery. I will provide monthly invoices.

Looking forward to getting started.

Best regards,
Dr. Robert Patel
records.sprk_description: Dr. Patel confirms engagement, proposes kickoff meeting, identifies precision alignment mechanism as critical focus area, and confirms $750/hour rate.

records.sprk_subject: Expert Report Draft — Initial Review Comments
records.sprk_body:
Dr. Patel,

Thank you for the draft expert report delivered on January 28. I have reviewed it with Rachel and we have the following comments:

1. Section 4.2 — The claim-by-claim analysis for Patent '567 is excellent and well-supported. However, we think you should add additional detail on the precision alignment mechanism comparison, specifically addressing how the AutoForge Mark III implementation maps to claim limitation 3(b).

2. Section 5.1 — Your damages methodology section needs strengthening. We suggest including the comparable license analysis from the TechPrecision v. ManuCorp case that we discussed.

3. Section 6 — The conclusion could be more definitive. Rather than stating the AutoForge system 'likely' infringes, we recommend stating your opinion to a reasonable degree of engineering certainty.

4. Minor formatting corrections are marked in the attached redline.

Please let us know if you can incorporate these revisions by February 10. We need to finalize before the claim construction hearing.

Best regards,
Michael Park
records.sprk_description: Michael Park provides detailed review comments on Dr. Patel's draft expert report, requesting revisions to claim analysis, damages methodology, and conclusion strength.

records.sprk_subject: RE: Expert Report Draft — Initial Review Comments
records.sprk_body:
Michael,

Thank you for the thorough feedback. I agree with all of your suggestions and will incorporate the revisions.

Regarding the AutoForge Mark III analysis in Section 4.2 — I have obtained additional technical documentation from the second production set that provides clear evidence of the precision alignment mechanism implementation. The correspondence between claim limitation 3(b) and the Mark III design is actually stronger than I initially assessed.

I can have the revised report ready by February 7, three days ahead of your deadline. This will give you time for a final review before the Markman hearing.

One question — should I also prepare a demonstrative exhibit for the claim construction hearing showing the element-by-element comparison? This could be very effective for Judge Sullivan.

Best regards,
Dr. Robert Patel
records.sprk_description: Dr. Patel agrees to incorporate review comments, reports stronger Mark III infringement evidence, and offers to prepare demonstrative exhibit for Markman hearing.

records.sprk_subject: Dr. Nakamura Invalidity Report — Analysis and Rebuttal Strategy
records.sprk_body:
Dr. Patel,

Attached please find Dr. Emily Nakamura's invalidity report served by Pinnacle on February 5. I need your analysis and rebuttal recommendations.

Her report identifies three prior art references that she claims anticipate or render obvious the asserted patent claims:

1. The Henderson 2019 paper on adaptive manufacturing alignment
2. Japanese Patent Application 2020-145832 (Yamada reference)
3. The ManuTech Conference presentation from April 2023

Our preliminary view is that none of these references are particularly strong, but the Henderson paper combined with the Yamada reference could present an obviousness argument that requires careful rebuttal.

Please review the report and prepare a memo outlining your recommended rebuttal approach. We would like to discuss on a call next week, tentatively February 12 at 2:00 PM Pacific.

Best regards,
Rachel Torres
records.sprk_description: Rachel Torres forwards Dr. Nakamura's invalidity report to Dr. Patel for analysis and rebuttal strategy development, identifying three prior art references of concern.

records.sprk_subject: RE: Dr. Nakamura Invalidity Report — Analysis and Rebuttal Strategy
records.sprk_body:
Rachel,

I have completed my initial review of Dr. Nakamura's invalidity report. Here are my preliminary observations:

The Henderson 2019 paper, while discussing adaptive alignment, addresses a fundamentally different mechanical approach. Henderson's method relies on servo-driven feedback loops, while the Meridian patents claim a passive precision alignment mechanism. The distinction is significant and I am confident we can rebut this reference effectively.

The Yamada reference is more concerning. However, the critical limitation in claims 1 and 3 — the 'multi-axis calibration protocol' — is not taught by Yamada. Dr. Nakamura's argument requires combining Yamada with Henderson, and I believe the motivation to combine is weak.

The ManuTech 2023 presentation is a red herring. It postdates the priority date of Patent '567 and is not prior art for claims 1-8.

I am available February 12 at 2:00 PM as you suggested. I will have a detailed rebuttal outline prepared by then.

Best regards,
Dr. Robert Patel
records.sprk_description: Dr. Patel provides preliminary analysis of invalidity references, finding Henderson and ManuTech references rebuttable but noting Yamada as more concerning. Confirms rebuttal call.

records.sprk_subject: Task Assignment — Document Review Batch 7 Prioritization
records.sprk_body:
Lisa,

We have received the damages discovery documents from Pinnacle — approximately 1,100 documents covering financial records, revenue reports, and licensing comparables.

I need you to prioritize the review as follows:

1. First priority: AutoForge revenue reports and sales data (tag as 'damages-revenue')
2. Second priority: Any licensing agreements Pinnacle has entered with third parties (tag as 'damages-licensing')
3. Third priority: Marketing materials showing product positioning and market share (tag as 'damages-market')

Please aim to have the first priority review completed by March 1 and the full batch by March 10. This data is critical for our damages expert's analysis.

Also, please flag any documents that appear to have been improperly redacted — there have been some issues with Pinnacle's redaction practices in prior productions.

Thanks,
Michael
records.sprk_description: Michael Park assigns Lisa Huang to review damages discovery documents with prioritized review schedule and tagging instructions.

records.sprk_subject: RE: Task Assignment — Document Review Batch 7 Prioritization
records.sprk_body:
Michael,

Understood. I will begin the damages discovery review immediately.

Quick update on current workload: I have completed the ESI forensic collection review (Batch 6) and identified 43 potentially relevant documents that were marked as deleted on the custodian devices. I have flagged these for your review — some appear to be internal communications about the AutoForge precision alignment feature that were deleted after the litigation hold was issued.

I will prioritize the damages batch as you have outlined. Expected completion of first priority by February 28 and full batch by March 8, slightly ahead of your deadline.

Regarding the redaction issues — I will create a separate log of any documents where redactions appear overbroad or inconsistent with the stated bases.

Thanks,
Lisa
records.sprk_description: Lisa Huang confirms review assignment, reports finding 43 potentially relevant deleted documents from ESI review, and commits to ahead-of-schedule completion.

records.sprk_subject: Trial Preparation — Initial Planning Discussion
records.sprk_body:
Rachel and Michael,

With fact discovery nearing completion and the Markman ruling in hand, I want to start laying groundwork for trial preparation.

Some items to discuss at our next team meeting:

1. Witness list — We need to finalize our list of fact and expert witnesses for trial. My preliminary list includes James Morrison, Dr. Patel, and possibly Sarah Chen as a damages witness.

2. Exhibit list — Lisa should begin compiling the trial exhibit list from our key document database. I estimate we will need 150-200 exhibits.

3. Jury instructions — We should begin drafting proposed jury instructions on infringement and damages standards. I have templates from our recent TechPrecision case.

4. Demonstratives — Dr. Patel has offered to help prepare technical demonstratives for the jury. We should take him up on this.

5. Trial timeline — Two-week trial starting June 30. We need to map out daily witness order and time allocation.

Can we schedule a trial prep kickoff meeting for the week of March 10?

Best,
Rachel
records.sprk_description: Rachel Torres initiates trial preparation planning, outlining witness list, exhibit list, jury instructions, demonstratives, and trial timeline needs.

records.sprk_subject: RE: Trial Preparation — Initial Planning Discussion
records.sprk_body:
Rachel,

Agreed on all points. I have started drafting the trial witness list and will have a preliminary version ready for the kickoff meeting.

A few additional items to add to the agenda:

6. Motions in limine — I have identified at least three evidentiary issues we should address pre-trial. Pinnacle will likely try to introduce their 'independent development' narrative, and we need to move to exclude certain testimony.

7. Jury consultant — Should we engage a jury consultant for voir dire? Given the technical nature of the case, juror comprehension could be a significant factor.

8. Settlement leverage — The trial prep kickoff itself may signal to opposing counsel that we are serious about going to trial. This could accelerate their Q2 settlement timeline.

I am available the week of March 10. Let me check Lisa's calendar and propose specific times.

Best,
Michael
records.sprk_description: Michael Park adds motions in limine, jury consultant, and settlement leverage to trial prep agenda and agrees to March 10 kickoff timeline.

records.sprk_subject: Scheduling Order — Meridian Corp v. Pinnacle Industries
records.sprk_body:
TO: All Counsel of Record
FROM: Chambers of Hon. Margaret Sullivan
RE: Case No. 3:2025-cv-04892 — Amended Scheduling Order

The Court issues this amended scheduling order following the status conference of September 22, 2025:

1. Initial Disclosures: October 1, 2025
2. Fact Discovery Cutoff: March 15, 2026
3. Expert Disclosure — Plaintiff: April 1, 2026
4. Expert Disclosure — Defendant: April 15, 2026
5. Expert Discovery Cutoff: April 15, 2026
6. Claim Construction Hearing: February 14, 2026
7. Dispositive Motions Deadline: May 1, 2026
8. Pre-Trial Conference: June 1, 2026
9. Trial: June 30, 2026 (two weeks)

All dates are firm. Extensions will be granted only upon showing of good cause. The parties are reminded of their continuing obligation to supplement discovery under Rule 26(e).

SO ORDERED.
Hon. Margaret Sullivan
United States District Judge
records.sprk_description: Court scheduling order setting all key litigation deadlines including discovery cutoffs, claim construction hearing, motions deadline, and trial date.

records.sprk_subject: Notice of Hearing — Motion to Compel Third-Party Subpoena Compliance
records.sprk_body:
TO: All Counsel of Record
FROM: Chambers of Hon. Margaret Sullivan
RE: Case No. 3:2025-cv-04892 — Notice of Hearing

Please take notice that Plaintiff's Motion to Compel Compliance with Third-Party Subpoenas (Dkt. 87) is set for hearing on February 28, 2026, at 10:00 AM in Courtroom 4, 17th Floor.

Opposition briefs are due February 14, 2026. Reply briefs are due February 21, 2026.

The hearing will be conducted in person. Telephonic appearances will not be permitted.

SO ORDERED.
Hon. Margaret Sullivan
United States District Judge
records.sprk_description: Court notice setting hearing date for motion to compel third-party subpoena compliance, with opposition and reply brief deadlines.

records.sprk_subject: Claim Construction Order — Meridian Corp v. Pinnacle Industries
records.sprk_body:
TO: All Counsel of Record
FROM: Chambers of Hon. Margaret Sullivan
RE: Case No. 3:2025-cv-04892 — Order on Claim Construction

Following the February 14, 2026 Markman hearing, the Court issues this order construing the disputed claim terms in the asserted patents.

The Court's constructions of the eight disputed terms are set forth in the attached Claim Construction Memorandum (32 pages). In summary:

- 'Precision alignment mechanism' — Construed as 'a mechanical system that positions components with sub-millimeter accuracy through passive alignment features, without requiring active servo-driven feedback.' (Plaintiff's proposed construction adopted.)
- 'Multi-axis calibration protocol' — Construed as 'a sequence of calibration steps performed across at least two spatial axes.' (Plaintiff's proposed construction adopted with minor modification.)

The full memorandum is available on the docket.

SO ORDERED.
Hon. Margaret Sullivan
United States District Judge
records.sprk_description: Court claim construction order adopting plaintiff's proposed constructions for key terms including 'precision alignment mechanism' and 'multi-axis calibration protocol.'

records.sprk_subject: Deadline Reminder — Joint Case Management Statement Due February 25
records.sprk_body:
TO: All Counsel of Record
FROM: Clerk of Court, Northern District of California
RE: Case No. 3:2025-cv-04892 — Joint Case Management Statement Reminder

This is an automated reminder that the Joint Case Management Statement is due on February 25, 2026, per the Court's standing order.

The statement should address:
1. Current status of discovery
2. Remaining depositions and discovery disputes
3. Anticipated motions
4. Settlement status
5. Readiness for trial

Please file the joint statement on CM/ECF by 5:00 PM Pacific.

Clerk of Court
Northern District of California
records.sprk_description: Automated court reminder for joint case management statement filing deadline, listing required topics to address.

records.sprk_subject: Expert Deposition Scheduling — Dr. Patel and Dr. Nakamura
records.sprk_body:
Dear Mr. Kim,

Following the completion of claim construction proceedings, we write to finalize the expert deposition schedule.

We propose the following dates:

1. Dr. Robert Patel (Plaintiff's Expert) — March 10, 2026 at Baker & Associates, San Francisco. We anticipate the deposition will require one full day (7 hours).

2. Dr. Emily Nakamura (Defendant's Expert) — March 20, 2026 at Chen Law Group, Austin. We similarly anticipate one full day.

These dates provide sufficient time before the April 15 expert discovery cutoff for any follow-up that may be needed.

Please confirm whether these dates are acceptable for your client and Dr. Nakamura. We are flexible on the exact dates within the March 5-25 window if adjustments are needed.

Sincerely,
Rachel Torres
Baker & Associates LLP
records.sprk_description: Rachel Torres proposes expert deposition dates for Dr. Patel (March 10) and Dr. Nakamura (March 20), both one full day, before expert discovery cutoff.
